title: PC Management Best Practices: A Study of the Total Cost of Operation, Risk, Security, and Audit
author: Mark Salamasick, Charles Le Grand
abstract: IIA Research Foundation rough-draft report (Oct 7, 2003) authored by Mark Salamasick (University of Texas at Dallas) and Charles Le Grand (IIA AVP Technology Practices). Based on 221 GAIN flash-survey responses plus the July 2003 IIA Technology Audit Forum where Peter Kastner of Aberdeen Group was keynote speaker. Organized around the COSO Internal Control Integrated Framework and SAC model; covers business risk and insurance, Sarbanes-Oxley/HIPAA/GLBA compliance, information security and appropriate use, business continuity, software management, and PC TCO. Cites Kastner's research extensively on PC fleet TCO ($8k-$9k per seat), 20-25% cost reduction through best practices, 70-80% of TCO in operations, 40 million desktops running outdated OSes, and standardized images.
importance_rationale: Landmark IIA Research Foundation publication that codified PC fleet audit practices on the eve of the Sarbanes-Oxley IT-controls wave; heavily features Kastner's Aberdeen Group research as keynote input.
relevance_rationale: COSO-structured PC controls, auditor role in PC fleet management, TCO methodology, and patch/image/standardization best practices remain foundational for IT audit and endpoint management practice.
prescience_rationale: Correctly framed Sarbanes-Oxley's demand for documented PC controls, anticipated wireless risk (only 21% had done wireless cost analysis at time of writing), flagged old-OS security crisis (Win95/98/NT4), and identified patch management as dominant risk vector (~90% of attacks target known vulnerabilities).
